caption: A screen capture shows a webpage from the Internal Revenue Service (IRS), indicated by the "IRS" logo in the top left. The page contains tax-related text stating that certain amounts can be "repaid over a 3-year period or have the taxes due on the distribution spread over three years." It further explains that a "2020 withdrawal from an inherited IRA could not be repaid to the inherited IRA but may be spread over three years for income inclusion," and provides a link to "Coronavirus Relief for Retirement Plans and IRAs pages." Additionally, it mentions that taxpayers can find "Frequently Asked Questions regarding Required Minimum Distributions" at IRS.gov. Navigation options for "SEARCH | HELP | MENU" appear at the top, and buttons for "Share" and "Print" are located at the bottom, next to the text "Page Last Reviewed or Updated: 12-Dec-2022." The visual remains static across the frames.
